options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of courses to return

list.usage_instructions: List courses owned by the authenticated instructor. Default limit is 100; use --limit/-l to change page size, --table/-t for table output, and --properties/-p such as `id,title,url` to reduce output. The --filter/-f flag exists for CLI consistency, but Udemy does not document server-side filters for this endpoint.

get.help: Get one instructor-taught course by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Get a single course by numeric COURSE_ID from the authenticated instructor's taught course list. Use --table/-t for a key/value table or --properties/-p to return selected fields such as `id,title,rating,url`.

management.help: Get browser-backed course management fields except curriculum.
management.usage_instructions: Run `udemy courses management --help` for exact arguments and options.

update.help: Update browser-backed course management fields except curriculum.

options.name: --file
options.help: JSON file containing course management fields to update

update.usage_instructions: Run `udemy courses update --help` for exact arguments and options.

courses.usage_instructions: Use `udemy courses` for instructor-taught course data. Authenticate first with `udemy auth login`; the list command supports --limit/-l, --table/-t, and --properties/-p.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Save the Udemy Instructor API bearer token interactively. Use --profile/-p PROFILE to save credentials to a named profile, and --force/-F to clear existing ephemeral auth state before prompting.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored credentials for the active profile. Use --profile/-p PROFILE to clear a specific profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Check whether credentials are saved and see the active profile. Use --profile/-p PROFILE to inspect another profile and --table/-t for tabular output.

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: This command is part of the shared auth surface and is not used for Udemy personal access token auth. Prefer `udemy auth login --force` when replacing the bearer token.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Verify the saved bearer token by making a lightweight Instructor API call. Use --profile/-p PROFILE for a named profile, --verbose/-v for profile/base URL details, and --table/-t for table output.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List configured profiles and identify the default. Use --table/-t for table output, --limit/-l to cap rows, --filter/-f with field:op:value syntax, and --properties/-p to select profile fields.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Show metadata for one profile by NAME. Use --table/-t for a key/value table.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a named profile file from `.env.example`. After creating NAME, run `udemy auth login --profile NAME` to save that profile's bearer token.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Make NAME the default profile used when no --profile/-p is supplied to auth commands. Use after `auth profiles create` and `auth login --profile NAME`.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a named profile and its profile data. Use --force/-F to skip the confirmation prompt; the default profile cannot be deleted until another profile is selected.

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `udemy auth profiles` to manage named `.env` credential profiles. These commands are nested under `auth`; do not use a top-level `profiles` command.

auth.usage_instructions: Use `udemy auth` to save, check, clear, and test the Udemy bearer token. Auth commands accept --profile/-p for named profiles, and `auth profiles` manages the profile files.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Clear all cached responses for the active profile. Use this when stale course data appears or after switching accounts.

cache.usage_instructions: Use `udemy cache` for local cache maintenance. Cache commands do not contact Udemy.

usage_instructions: Use `udemy` for Udemy Instructor API work, especially listing instructor-taught courses, reading a specific course by ID, and managing bearer-token auth profiles. JSON is the default output; use --table/-t for readable tables and --properties/-p to select fields on course and profile list commands.
